response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished individual contributor with broad full-stack experience and genuine exposure to developer tooling (CI/CD setup, test frameworks, MCP generators), but the role requires a people manager with demonstrated team leadership and organizational change experience. The resume contains no evidence of direct reports, performance management, headcount planning, or organizational leadership — the core of this Level II Manager position. His technical profile, while strong, skews toward product engineering rather than the deep build systems and testing infrastructure focus the role demands. The mobile platform knowledge is thin and dated.
strengths: Long and diverse engineering career (20 years) spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Tangible developer tooling contributions: CI/CD pipelines, test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), and recent MCP/agentic tooling at Naver
Community knowledge-sharing through workshops, open-source projects, and conference talks signals mentorship instinct
concerns: Zero evidence of people management: no direct reports, no performance reviews, no hiring or career development ownership
No organizational or team-level leadership scope; all roles are individual contributor, even at 'Staff' level
Mobile platform depth is minimal and dated (Apple TV/iOS at WatchUp, 2015–2016), far short of the ecosystem-level expertise implied by the role
